headline: Oaktree's Howard Marks says there's no systemic problem with private credit
summary: Marks said any risk stems from the pace of expansion in direct lending, which has ballooned to a market now exceeding $1 trillion since inception around 2011.

headline: Trump, Senate Democrats want to curb corporate homebuyers—but it may not make houses easier to buy
summary: Proposed restrictions on large corporate homebuyers could affect less than 1% of total U.S. home sales, limiting their impact on overall housing affordability.

headline: Blackstone Redemptions Test Liquidity As Shares Trade Below Fair Value
summary: Blackstone's flagship private credit fund, BCRED, has seen a sharp rise in investor redemption requests. In response, Blackstone raised the fund's repurchase cap and injected additional capital to meet all redemption demands. The decision to fully honor requests and support the fund with fresh capital marks an unusual move in the private credit market. For investors watching NYSE:BX, this comes at a time when the share price is around $114.14 and recent stock performance has been weak, with...

headline: Is Blackstone (BX) Now At A Reasonable Price After Recent Share Price Slide
summary: If you are wondering whether Blackstone's current share price reflects its true worth, you are not alone. This article is going to focus squarely on what you might be paying for each dollar of value in the business. Blackstone's share price closed at US$114.14, with a 3.5% decline over 7 days, a 19.2% decline over 30 days, a 28.1% decline year to date and a 22.4% decline over 1 year, set against longer term returns of 36.9% over 3 years and 91.3% over 5 years. Recent headlines around...
